data.title: Africa Energy enters deal with Arostyle to restructure joint investment in Main Street 1549 Proprietary
data.description:
• Africa Energy (OTCPK:HPMCF) has entered into a non-binding agreement with Arostyle Investments Proprietary Ltd. to restructure their joint investment in Main Street 1549 Proprietary Ltd.

  • The firms, via Main Street 1549 Proprietary, presently hold a direct 10% participating stake in Block 11B/12B off the coast of South Africa.

  • The company currently owns 49% of the common shares and 100% of Class B shares of Main Street, while Arostyle holds the remaining 51% of the common shares.

  • Following the joint venture partners' withdrawal announced on July 29, and pending regulatory approvals, Main Street plans to take full ownership of the Block 11B/12B project.

  • Under the latest pact, Main Street is anticipated to be restricted as Africa Energy will hold a 75% direct interest in Block 11B/12B, while Arostyle will hold 25%.

  • Both parties aim to finalize the restructuring details by January 31.

data.title: Aadi Bioscience rockets 72% on key updates
data.description:
• Aadi Bioscience (NASDAQ:AADI) stock price soared 72% on Thursday during after market hours of trading as it signed an exclusive license agreement for Three-Asset ADC portfolio developed through a collaboration between WuXi Biologics (OTCPK:WXXWY) and HANGZHOU DAC.

  • The company will also sell FYARRO and associated infrastructure to KAKEN Pharmaceutical (OTCPK:KKPCF) for $100 million and also get PIPE financing of $100 million.

  • The capital will be used to fund operations into late 2028, including anticipated clinical data for the ADC portfolio.

  • Co-Founder and Former CEO of ProfoundBio, Baiteng Zhao, is appointed to Aadi (AADI) Board of Directors.

More on Aadi Bioscience

data.title: Walmart likely to miss targets for cutting greenhouse gas emissions
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) said it will likely miss its targets for reducing greenhouse gas emissions, citing challenges related to energy policy, infrastructure and the availability of cost-effective low-carbon technologies.

The retail giant was targeting to lower its greenhouse gas emissions by 35% by 2025 and by 65% by 2030 (from base year 2015), with the goal of achieving zero emissions across its operations by 2040.

Walmart (NYSE:WMT) noted that operational emissions decreased 19.3% as of the end of 2023 relative to 2015. But emissions increased 3.9% year-over-year in 2023 due to usage of aging, high-emitting refrigeration equipment, transportation emissions in the U.S. and a growing fleet, as well as renewable energy expansion slowing relative to business growth.

"While we continue to work toward our aspirational target of zero operational emissions by 2040, progress will not be linear and depends not only on our own initiatives, but also on factors beyond our control," the company updated on its website.

"These factors include energy policy and infrastructure around the world, availability of more cost-effective low-GWP (global warming potential) refrigeration and HVAC solutions, and timely emergence of cost-effective technologies for low-carbon heavy tractor transportation - which does not appear likely until the 2030s."

Even so, 48% of Walmart's (WMT) global electricity needs are supplied by renewable sources in 2023, in line with targets. It also achieved its Project Gigaton goal six years early, with suppliers reporting projects that are expected to exceed 1B metric tons of cumulative emissions reduced, avoided, or sequestered in global value chains by 2030.

"We continue to prioritize reductions in value chain emissions, including through the continuation of Project Gigaton," it said.

data.title: Banxa to complete going private transaction
data.description:
• Banxa (OTCPK:BNXAF) entered an agreement with 1493819 B.C. Ltd, a private company existing under the laws of British Columbia, to acquire all of shares in the capital of Banxa (OTCPK:BNXAF), other than those shares held by shareholders comprised of certain directors and executive officers for cash consideration of C$1.00 per share.
  • The consideration represents a 33% premium to the closing price of the shares on TSXV on December 18, 2024, the last trading day immediately prior to the announcement of the transaction, and a 16% and 54% premium, respectively, to the 30-day and 60-day average trading prices of the shares ending on December 18, 2024.
  • The continuing shareholders, collectively, beneficially own or control an aggregate of 26,407,990 shares (representing ~50% of outstanding shares on a non-diluted basis).

data.title: Retail sales in the U.K. rises less than expected
data.description:
• Retail Sales in the United Kingdom increased 0.20% in November of 2024 over the previous month, lower than market consensus of 0.50%.

  • Y/Y retail sales was up 0.50% in November, vs. 0.80% forecast.

  • ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

    Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Japan’s Nov headline inflation rate rises to three-month high of 2.9%; core inflation tops forecast
data.description:
• The annual inflation rate in Japan climbed to 2.9% in November 2024 from 2.3% in the prior month, marking the highest reading since October 2023.
  • The core inflation rate rose to a 3-month high of 2.7% in November, up from 2.3% in October and surpassing estimates of 2.6%, marking the highest core inflation rate in three months, following gains of 2.4% and 2.3% in September and October, respectively.
  • Monthly, the CPI increased by 0.6%, the highest figure in 13 months.
  • Food prices in Japan increased by 4.8% from a year earlier in November 2024, following a 3.5% rise in the prior month, marking the highest food inflation since March.
  • Earlier this week, the Bank of Japan held interest rates steady as policymakers needed time to assess wage trends, uncertainties of overseas economies and the next US administration's policies.

  • The Nikkei 225 Index climbed 0.3% to above 38,900, while the Topix Index gained 0.15% to 2,717 on Friday, as the yen slid to a five-month low, losing around 5% over the past two weeks. The Japanese yen strengthened past 157 per dollar on Friday, strong inflation data.

    More on Japan

data.title: Asia markets mixed as investors assess China rate decision; eyes on PCE inflation data
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mostly lower on Friday as investors reacted to the monthly fixing of key lending rates in China, with the PBoC maintaining them at record lows, as expected, after reductions in October and July.

Investors also responded to data showing Japan’s headline inflation rate rose to a three-month high of 2.9% in November, up from 2.3% in October.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell -0.17% to above 38,900, while the Topix Index gained 0.15% to 2,717 on Friday. The Japanese yen strengthened past 157 per dollar on Friday as investors responded to data showing Japan’s headline inflation rate rose to a three-month high of 2.9% in November, up from 2.3% in October. The core inflation rate also increased to 2.7%, surpassing market expectations of 2.6%.

China (SHCOMP) fell -0.14% to around 3,380 and the Shenzhen Component rising 0.4% to 10,690, and the offshore yuan stabilized at 7.30 per dollar after the People's Bank of China held its key lending rates steady at December's fixing.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +0.08% to 19,874 in Friday morning deals, after closing modestly lower in the previous session.

India (SENSEX) fell -0.22% to 79,090 in morning trade on Thursday, marking its fifth consecutive session of losses, while lingering at its lowest level in three weeks, driven primarily by declines in financial services, banking sectors, and realty stocks.

Australia (AS51) fell -1.24% to 8,067 on Friday, closing at its lowest level in over three months.

Private sector credit in Australia rose by 0.5% month-over-month in November 2024, in line with market consensus.

Domestically, investors turned their attention to the upcoming release of the Reserve Bank of Australia’s meeting minutes, following its decision to keep rates unchanged at 4.35%.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended little unchanged following the previous day's selloff as investors evaluated the potential impact of a hawkish Federal Reserve outlook on corporate returns in the coming year.

U.S. stock futures were little changed on Friday as investors braced for the upcoming PCE price index report, the Federal Reserve’s preferred measure of inflation: Dow -0.15%; S&P 500 -0.27%; Nasdaq -0.57%.

Traders also anticipated the release of the US PCE price index for November later today, the Fed’s preferred inflation measure.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

People's Bank of China keeps key lending rates steady for second straight month, as expected

Bank of Japan keeps policy rate unchanged at 0.25%, as widely expected

Japan's Nov export growth accelerates to three-month high, while imports unexpectedly fall

China's Nov retail sales slows as stimulus impact fades; unemployment rate held steady

Japan's factory activity shrinks for sixth straight month, services activity improves in December
